title: Contract: CVL Economics for a Study of the Economic Impact of the Arts in Berkeley

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any
necessary amendments with CVL Economics for an amount not to exceed $119,300 for
analysis of pandemic cultural arts recovery investments and the economic impact of the
arts in Berkeley for two-year term.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The $119,300 contract will be funded with American Rescue Act Funds designated for
the arts (Budget Code 354-21-208-252-0000-000-446-612990).
sections.current_situation:
CVL Economics was selected through a competitive Request for Proposals (RFP)
process to conduct an analysis of pandemic recovery investments and the economic
impact of the arts generally in Berkeley. The selected consultant has a demonstrated
record of success in providing economic analysis services and accessible impact
reports to municipalities and CVL Economics has specific relevant expertise in
economic analysis of the arts and culture sector. The contract scope of work includes
conducting comprehensive economic analyses and presenting them in an engaging
manner with the following four deliverables:
1. A written assessment of both the short-term and long-term impacts of the City of
Berkeley’s investment of both General Funds and American Rescue Plan Act
(ARPA) funds in the Berkeley Arts Sector through the City of Berkeley’s relief and
recovery grant programs (April 2020 & February 2022).
2. A baseline study of the current economic impact of the arts in Berkeley. This
study will assess the economic effects of and recovery from the pandemic to the
present on the City of Berkeley’s arts sector. This study will also provide data
suitable for an update to the Economic Impact of the Arts in Berkeley report
commissioned by the City of Berkeley in 2017.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 4

3. The project will culminate in a comprehensive report of both the COVID-19 relief
investment analysis, and the baseline economic impact study for public
distribution.
4. The development of a set of standard arts and culture economic impact metrics
and tools for future use by the City for the purpose of including arts and culture
economic impact data in the Office of Economic Development’s annual
“Economic Dashboard” reports, allowing the City
sections.background:
The City of Berkeley is home to over 150 active arts and culture nonprofits offering a
diverse array of theater, music, festivals, dance, visual arts, and more. The nonprofits
that make up Berkeley’s arts sector are particularly diverse in terms of their size,
educational offerings, and creative disciplines, and include numerous acclaimed
theaters, music venues, galleries, dance studios, and maker spaces. Many
organizations are nationally recognized, and the City is celebrated for its cultural vitality.
Complementing these cultural organizations, the City is also home to a wide range of
independent artists and artisans, who operate on a smaller scale producing fine arts
such as painting, sculpture, music, and crafts including but not limited to textiles, stained
glass, and metalwork. The City of Berkeley has long recognized the importance of the
arts and culture to the economic health, vitality, and diversity of Berkeley’s local
economy and its commercial districts. Local arts and cultural organizations generate
revenue, create jobs, and improve the quality of life, which in turn generates businesses
revenue and tourism to Berkeley. The City of Berkeley last conducted an analysis of the
economic impact of the arts in 2017, which was incorporated into the City of Berkeley’s
Arts & Culture Plan Update (2018 - 2027).
In response to the COVID-19 pandemic’s economic impacts on the arts sector, the City
of Berkeley made substantial funding allocations at two different times to provide
emergency relief and recovery funding to arts organizations, festivals, and artists in
Berkeley.
1. Emergency Business and Arts Continuity Grants – $3M, April 2020
In April of 2020, the City awarded grants to businesses and arts organizations
whose revenues were significantly impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic. The
Business and Arts Organization Continuity Grant Programs were funded through
a $3M allocation of the City’s catastrophic reserve fund on March 17, 2020. Of
that funding, $1 million
sections.rationale:
CVL Economics possesses demonstrated expertise centered on a range of regional
economic development matters, including fostering the creative economy and nonprofit
sustainability, understanding the economics of arts and culture, conducting public policy
analyses and presenting data in an engaging and accessible manner.
sections.alternatives:
The City received and evaluated twelve proposals in response to RFP Specification No.
24-11671-C which was posted on July 23, 2024. After proposal review, and team
interviews, the CVL Economics proposal received the highest score of the twelve
proposals.
CONTACT PERSON
Jennifer Lovvorn, Office of Economic Development, (510) 981-7533
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: CVL ECONOMICS FOR ANALYSIS OF PANDEMIC RECOVERY
INVESTMENTS AND THE ECONOMIC IMPACT OF THE ARTS IN BERKELEY
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley has long recognized the importance of the arts and
culture to the economic health, vitality, and diversity of Berkeley’s local economy and its
commercial districts; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley made two major funding allocations in response to the
COVID-19 pandemic’s economic impacts on the arts sector to provide emergency relief
and recovery funding to arts organizations, festivals, and artists in Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, Berkeley’s arts and culture sector continues to experience lower audience
levels, and increased costs in the aftermath of the pandemic, with some nonprofits
operating at a loss; and
WHEREAS, an impact study is fundamental to establishing a new baseline of data related
to the economic impact of the arts and culture sector in Berkeley and to analyze the effect
of the City’s investments in the arts during the pandemic; and
WHEREAS, the City released an RFP under Specification No. 24-11671-C for which
twelve proposals were received, evaluated and scored, resulting in the selection of CVL
Economics to conduct an analysis of pandemic recovery investments and the economic
impact of the arts in Berkeley.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments thereto with CVL
Economics for analysis of pandemic recovery investments and the economic impact of
the arts in Berkeley, in an amount not to exceed $119,300 for a term of two years. A
record signature copy of said contract and any amendments are to be on file in the Office
of the City Clerk.
